zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Radcliffe於大約1454年出生在英國藍卡爾時，她的父親Sir John Radcliffe26歲，母親Isabel Tyldesley25歲。她於大約1475年與Sir Nicholas Rishton在英國藍卡爾登肯哈爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1528年7月6日在英國藍卡爾克雷頓 勒 波斯享年75歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Radcliffe于大约1454年出生在英国藍卡爾时，她的父亲Sir John Radcliffe26岁，母亲Isabel Tyldesley25岁。她于大约1475年与Sir Nicholas Rishton在英国藍卡爾登肯哈爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1528年7月6日在英国藍卡爾克雷頓 勒 波斯享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: Radcliffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ratliff
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from one or more of the places so named, such as Radcliffe (Lancashire), Radcliffe on Trent and Ratcliffe on Soar (both Nottinghamshire), Radclive (Buckinghamshire), Ratcliffe Culey and Ratcliffe on the Wreake (both Leicestershire), Ratclyffe in Clyst Hydon, Ratcliffes in Thorverton, and Ratcliffes in Broad Clyst (all Devon), Ratcliff in Stepney (Middlesex), and Rackley in Compton Bishop (Somerset). The placenames derive from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’ (see also Rutley ). A family of the name Radcliffe trace their descent from Sir Nicholas de Radclyffe. He is said to have been a knight who held the major of Radcliffe in Lancashire and served Roger de Poitou, Baron de Marsey in the 11th century.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Radcliffe was born about 1454, in Lancashire, England, her father, Sir John Radcliffe, was 26 and her mother, Isabel Tyldesley, was 25. She married Sir Nicholas Rishton about 1475, in Dunkenhalgh, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 6 July 1528, in Clayton le Moors, Lancashire, England, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Radcliffe est née environ 1454, à Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Radcliffe, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Isabel Tyldesley, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Nicholas Rishton environ 1475, à Dunkenhalgh, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 6 juillet 1528, à Clayton le Moors, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Radcliffeが約1454年にイギリスランカシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir John Radcliffeは26歳，母Isabel Tyldesleyは25歳であった。約1475年にイギリスランカシュアイアダンケンハルグでSir Nicholas Rishtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1528年7月6日にイギリスランカシュアイアクレイトン ル ムールズで75歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Radcliffe(이)가 대략 1454년 영국 랭카실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Radcliffe(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Tyldesley(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1475년 영국 랭카실르 던켄할그h에서 Sir Nicholas Rishton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1528년 7월 6일 영국 랭카실르 클레이턴 일 무르스에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Radcliffe родилась около 1454 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Radcliffe было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Tyldesley, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Nicholas Rishton около 1475 г. в местности дункенэлг, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 6 июля 1528 г. в местности клеитон л мурс, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 75 лет.
